question: Which product categories drove the most revenue last month?
sql: SELECT p.category AS category, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue, COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) AS orders FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') AND DATE_TRUNC(DATE(oi.created_at), MONTH) = DATE_TRUNC(DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), INTERVAL 1 MONTH), MONTH) GROUP BY category ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 10
analyst_report: Rank categories by realized order item sale price, exclude cancelled and returned items, and show order count as context so managers do not overreact to a single expensive item.

question: Why is a branch underperforming compared with another branch?
sql: SELECT u.state AS region, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS orders, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS revenue, ROUND(AVG(oi.sale_price), 2) AS avg_item_price, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN oi.status IN ('Cancelled','Returned') THEN oi.sale_price ELSE 0 END), 2) AS lost_revenue FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.orders` AS o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.users` AS u ON o.user_id = u.id GROUP BY region ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 20
analyst_report: The public dataset has customer geography rather than store branches, so use region as the comparison unit. Diagnose underperformance through order volume, average item price, and cancelled/returned revenue rather than revenue alone.

question: Who are our top customers by spend?
sql: SELECT o.user_id AS customer_id, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS orders, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS total_spend FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.orders` AS o JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id WHERE oi.status NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned') GROUP BY customer_id ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 10
analyst_report: Use customer IDs only. Never show names, emails, phone numbers, or addresses. Add order count so the executive can distinguish loyal customers from one-off high spenders.

question: Which products sell well but have high return risk?
sql: SELECT p.name AS product_name, p.category AS category, COUNT(*) AS items_sold, ROUND(SUM(oi.sale_price), 2) AS gross_sales, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(CASE WHEN oi.status = 'Returned' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), COUNT(*)), 3) AS return_rate FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` AS oi JOIN `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.products` AS p ON oi.product_id = p.id GROUP BY product_name, category HAVING items_sold >= 20 ORDER BY return_rate DESC, gross_sales DESC LIMIT 15
analyst_report: For merchandising decisions, combine demand and quality signals. Filter out tiny sample sizes, then rank by return rate and gross sales to find products that need attention.

question: What data can I ask questions about?
sql: SELECT 'orders' AS table_name UNION ALL SELECT 'order_items' UNION ALL SELECT 'products' UNION ALL SELECT 'users' LIMIT 4
analyst_report: Explain the business concepts, not just table names: orders cover purchases, order_items cover item-level sales and status, products cover catalog attributes, and users cover customer demographics with PII restrictions.
